name: Cerro de la Cruz de Hinojosas Trail
name_original: Subida a la Cruz de Hinojosas — circular desde Hinojosas de Calatrava
description: Hike the moderate 8.6-mile Ascent to the Cruz de Hinojosas circular from Hinojosas de Calatrava for panoramic views and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Puertollano offers diverse outdoor opportunities, characterized by unique geological formations, historical landscapes, and natural features. The region features ancient fossilized forests at the Carboniferous Natural Monument, showcasing prehistoric wetlands. Volcanic landscapes, such as the Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo, add to the varied terrain. The nearby Sierra Morena de Ciudad Real provides a backdrop for more extensive outdoor pursuits. Best hiking trails around Puertollano The most popular hiking route is Cerro de la Cruz de Hinojosas Trail, a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and diverse terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is San José Parish – Doctor Fleming Square loop from Puertollano, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This trail features urban green spaces and views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Fuente Agria – Paseo de San Gregorio loop from Puertollano, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through the landscaped Paseo de San Gregorio and past the Fuente Agria, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Puertollano is defined by ancient fossilized forests, volcanic features, and the expansive Sierra Morena. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Puertollano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Puertollano?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Puertollano, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 15 easy routes, 20 moderate options, and 2 more challenging trails to explore.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Puertollano offers 15 easy hiking routes. A good option for a gentler experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria – Paseo de San Gregorio loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.4 km with a modest 110 meters of elevation gain, passing through landscaped areas and by the famous Fuente Agria.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Puertollano area?
A: Many of the hiking trails around Puertollano are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197180191/cerro-de-la-cruz-de-hinojosas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cruz de Hinojosas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 13.8 km loop offering panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in unique features. You can explore the ancient fossilized forests at the Carboniferous Natural Monument, or witness volcanic landscapes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria&lt;/a&gt;, known for its iron-rich waters, is also a prominent natural landmark. Additionally, the imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent&lt;/a&gt; offers historical interest and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puertollano?
A: The most pleasant times for hiking in Puertollano are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, making for comfortable and scenic walks.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Puertollano suitable for dogs?
A: While many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific local regulations for each route, especially concerning nature reserves or protected areas. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in this region?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33812465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San José Parish – Doctor Fleming Square loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.9 km, typically take around 3.5 to 4 hours to complete. Shorter, easier routes will take less time, while longer or more challenging ones will require more.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Puertollano?
A: Puertollano has local bus services that may connect to areas near some trailheads, especially those closer to urban centers. For trails further afield, personal transport is often more convenient. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes for specific access points.

Q: Where can I park when starting a hike in Puertollano?
A: Many trailheads, particularly those on the outskirts of Puertollano or leading to natural areas, often have designated parking spaces or roadside parking available. For routes starting within the town, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19409754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doctor Fleming Square – Puertollano loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can usually find public parking within the urban area.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse landscapes around Puertollano, including the Sierra Morena, are home to various wildlife. You might spot birds of prey, small mammals, and a range of reptiles and amphibians, especially near water sources. The Carboniferous Natural Monument offers a unique glimpse into ancient flora and fauna through its fossilized remains.

Q: Are there any accessible hiking trails for wheelchair users?
A: While the region offers many trails, specific information on wheelchair-accessible routes is limited. Most trails involve natural terrain, which may not be suitable. It's recommended to look for paved paths within urban parks or specific accessible nature trails if available.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about the trails in Puertollano?
A: The hiking trails in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the unique geological formations like the ancient fossilized forests, and the panoramic views offered on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5298543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Hinojosas de Calatrava – The Rollers loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Route that as its name indicates is a set of ups and downs that go in the direction, if you want, to the mountain of La Cruz.
From this point you can see the entire town of Hinojosas de Calatrava and the fields and mountain slopes that surround it!

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Ascent to the Cruz de Hinojosas — circular from Hinojosas de Calatrava?
faqs.answer: The hike is approximately 13.8 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it features a gradual ascent with continuous elevation gain, the descent includes a "sinuous and quite steep" path. Some sections near the summit may require walking in single file, indicating narrower or more challenging parts.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Ascent to the Cruz de Hinojosas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Hinojosas de Calatrava. You can typically find parking within the village itself, near the trailhead, as it's a circular route originating from there. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or easily identifiable upon arrival in the village.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any specific permits to hike the Ascent to the Cruz de Hinojosas trail, nor are there any entrance fees. The trail is freely accessible to the public. It is located within the broader area of the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, which generally does not require permits for hiking on established trails.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to the Cruz de Hinojosas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of native vegetation and potential wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year, but spring and autumn are often ideal. During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the landscape is particularly vibrant with blooming flora or rich autumnal colors. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's best to start early in the morning.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins on a track, transitioning to a path through olive groves. As you gain elevation, the landscape shifts to native vegetation. Near the summit, it becomes a more winding path. The descent is described as "sinuous and quite steep," running parallel to a large scree slope, offering a diverse hiking experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the summit with the Cruz de Hinojosas de Calatrava, where three crosses stand. You'll also enjoy excellent panoramic views, including the View of Hinojosas de Calatrava, the Valle de Alcudia, Sierra Madrona, and further along the ridge, the Ojailén Valley and Puertollano. Don't miss The Rollers, a notable trail section.

faqs.question: What unique flora can be found on the trail?
faqs.answer: As you ascend, the landscape features native vegetation such as rockroses, gorse, rosemary, junipers, holm oaks, and French lavender. You might also spot arbutus trees, known for their delightful fruits, and some cork oaks along the way.

faqs.question: Does this trail pass through any other notable paths or protected areas?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is situated within the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. While primarily following its own path, sections of this circular trail briefly intersect with other local routes such as "camino alto de semda de la calera" and "Cordel_alcoba".

faqs.question: What makes the Cruz de Hinojosas summit special?
faqs.answer: The summit of the Cruz de Hinojosas de Calatrava is marked by three crosses: one large concrete cross and two smaller iron ones. Additionally, there's a small cavity under a rock nearby where locals often leave mementos, adding a unique cultural touch to the natural landmark.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Hinojosas de Calatrava, a village where you can find basic amenities. While specific cafes or pubs on the trail itself are unlikely, you can typically find local establishments in the village for refreshments or a meal before or after your hike. Accommodation options might also be available in or near Hinojosas de Calatrava.
